question: What was the trip date?
Who is the school principal?
reference_answer: Dr. Imani Adeyemi.

question: Who is the school nurse?
reference_answer: Karen Tomic, RN.

question: Who substituted for Mrs. Pruitt as chaperone on the day of the trip?
reference_answer: Ms. Hattie Yi, school librarian.

question: What is the total student roster size for the trip?
reference_answer: 79 students (38 in 7A, 41 in 7B).

question: Who drove Bus 2?
reference_answer: Karenna Adekoya (Hamilton-Worthing Transit, vehicle HWT-209).

question: Who is the homeroom teacher for class 7A?
reference_answer: Ms. Beatriz Calderon.

question: What is the name of the venue's RFID wristband management system?
reference_answer: Cranberry Hollow Visitor Management System v.5.2.

question: Which exhibit is the venue wristband scan log?
reference_answer: Exhibit V-1.

question: Which exhibit is the nurse's note about Devin Rodriguez?
reference_answer: Exhibit N-1.

question: Which exhibit is Calderon's teacher statement?
reference_answer: Exhibit S-1.

question: Which exhibit is the parent letter from the Marchetti family?
reference_answer: Exhibit P-1.

question: According to whose statement was Lila observed walking toward the bus loading area at approximately 2:33 PM?
reference_answer: Davis Whitaker's statement (Exhibit S-2).

question: Which source records the Bus 2 student loading count of 41 at 8:25 AM?
reference_answer: Bus 2 driver Karenna Adekoya's manifest log.

question: As of the day of the trip, who was on the chaperone roster of record for Bus 2?
reference_answer: Lead chaperone Mr. Davis Whitaker; parent/other chaperones Drummond, Boateng, Halloran, Petrov, and Ms. Yi (substituting for Pruitt). Nurse Karen Tomic was also assigned to Bus 2.

question: Was Pruitt on the chaperone roster of record on the day of the trip?
reference_answer: No. Pruitt called out sick at 7:10 AM on April 24 and was replaced by Yi at 7:35 AM. The April 22/April 23 newsletter version naming Pruitt is superseded.

question: After the morning-of bus swaps, how many students were on Bus 1?
reference_answer: 38 students: 37 from 7A plus Aliyah Saint-Pierre (7B).

question: In what specific way was the pre-swap bus manifest superseded by the post-swap manifest of record?
reference_answer: The post-swap manifest replaced Marcus Donovan (7A, moved to Bus 2) with Aliyah Saint-Pierre (7B, moved to Bus 1) on Bus 1, and made the inverse change on Bus 2. Total counts on each bus remained the same (38 on Bus 1, 41 on Bus 2), but the homeroom composition of each bus changed.

question: Did the April 18 draft trip plan govern lunch supervision arrangements?
reference_answer: No. The April 18 draft was revised on April 20 and superseded by the April 22 final plan; it is retained for procedural records only.

question: As of 2:35 PM at the bus reload, what was the headcount discrepancy?
reference_answer: Bus 2 was one student short, with Bus 2 driver Adekoya counting 40 instead of the expected 41.

question: As of 12:35 PM at the lunch table check, what was the confirmed student count?
reference_answer: 79 students.

question: As of 1:30 PM, where was Devin Rodriguez and who was supervising him?
reference_answer: Devin was at the Welcome Center, supervised by Ms. Hattie Yi. He sat out the Schoolhouse Demonstration from 1:30 to 2:30, when he rejoined the group for bus reload.

question: As of 2:38 PM, had Lila Marchetti been located?
reference_answer: No. She was located at 2:42 PM at the Mill Station restroom area.

question: As of packet date, has the May 1 protocol review been held?
reference_answer: No. Scheduled for May 1, 2026; not yet held as of the April 28 packet date.

question: Per district policy FT-2024.3 §6.4, what is the required chaperone-to-student ratio for grade 7–8 field trips?
reference_answer: 1:10 or better.

question: Per FT-2024.3 §7.2, what is required at each major program transition?
reference_answer: Per-student check-in/check-out at each major program transition.

question: Was the trip's chaperone-to-student ratio (excluding the nurse) within policy?
reference_answer: Yes. With 12 chaperones (excluding the nurse) for 79 students, the ratio of approximately 1:6.58 is within the 1:10 policy.

question: Per the chaperone roster of record on the day of the trip, how many adults were on the trip?
reference_answer: 13 adults: 12 chaperones plus 1 nurse (Tomic).

question: Who has authority to determine whether the schoolhouse-demo supervision arrangement met district policy?
reference_answer: Principal Dr. Imani Adeyemi, in conjunction with the building leadership team via the May 1 review.

question: How many parent chaperones were on the chaperone roster as of April 22, 2026?
reference_answer: 10 parent chaperones: 5 on Bus 1 (Vargas, Mitchell, Goldberg, Echeverria, Ryker) and 5 on Bus 2 (Pruitt, Drummond, Boateng, Halloran, Petrov).

question: What was the elapsed time between Bus 2's first short headcount at 2:35 PM and the final confirmed headcount?
reference_answer: 10 minutes (2:35 PM to 2:45 PM).

question: List the wristband scan events for Lila Marchetti between 2:30 PM and 2:45 PM.
reference_answer: Three events: 2:33 PM Schoolhouse exit; 2:35 PM Mill Station entry; 2:42 PM Mill Station exit.

question: How many distinct stations does the wristband scan log show Lila visited during the trip (Welcome through bus reload)?
reference_answer: Five distinct stations: Welcome Center, Mill Station, Farm Station, Pavilion, and Schoolhouse. Mill Station was visited twice (10:32–11:30 and 2:35–2:42), but counts once.

question: Has the school administration determined whether the schoolhouse-demo supervision arrangement met district policy?
reference_answer: No. Pending the May 1 review by the building leadership team.

question: Has it been determined whether the schoolhouse-demo to bus reload transition is a "major program transition" under FT-2024.3 §7.2?
reference_answer: No. The packet states this is one of the questions referred to the May 1 review.

question: Has the parent letter from the Marchetti family received a substantive determination?
reference_answer: No. Adeyemi acknowledged receipt April 30 and confirmed the May 1 review will address the protocol questions; no determination has been issued as of packet date.

question: Does the packet clarify whether the nurse counts toward the chaperone-to-student ratio?
reference_answer: No. The packet's open-items list flags this question as 'referenced but not clarified in current packet.'

question: Whitaker's statement says he observed Lila walking toward the bus loading area at 2:33 PM. The wristband scan log shows Lila scanned into Mill Station at 2:35 PM. How is this reconciled?
reference_answer: Whitaker's observation at 2:33 PM was approximately at the moment Lila exited the Schoolhouse (the scan log records Schoolhouse exit at 2:33). The wristband log records Mill Station entry at 2:35; in the intervening two minutes, Lila diverged from the group toward the Mill Station restroom (the nearest restroom to the Schoolhouse). Whitaker's statement reflects an instantaneous observation; the scan log records her subsequent path.

question: The April 23 parent newsletter named Pruitt as a Bus 2 chaperone. The day-of chaperone roster names Yi instead. Which is currently authoritative, and why?
reference_answer: The day-of chaperone roster (with Yi) is currently authoritative. Pruitt called out at 7:10 AM April 24, was replaced by Yi at 7:35 AM, and the April 23 newsletter version naming Pruitt is superseded.

question: The Bus 2 driver's manual count at 2:35 PM was 40, indicating one student short. The wristband scan log places Lila Marchetti at Mill Station entry at 2:35 PM. How are these two records reconciled?
reference_answer: There is no contradiction. The Bus 2 driver's count at 2:35 of 40 students reflects that one student (Lila Marchetti) was not at the bus. The wristband scan log places Lila at Mill Station entry at the same time, corroborating her absence from the bus area. Both records consistently identify Lila as the student missing from the bus reload count.
